top_signals.title: ‘Between supportive and restrictive forces’: Bitcoin stalls near $64,000 as Fed rate-hike risk overshadows Iran ceasefire relief
top_signals.summary: Bitcoin holds near $64,000 as six weeks of ETF outflows, and a hawkish Fed overshadow the U.S.-Iran peace deal.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Kuwait Offers Gulf Oil Loadings as Producers Seek Hormuz Outlet
top_signals.summary: Kuwait is offering naphtha for loading at its ports deep into the Persian Gulf in the first such tender in months, as Middle Eastern oil producers seek to raise shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. State-held Kuwait Petroleum Corporation (KPC) has issued a tender to sell naphtha cargoes to be picked up at Kuwaiti ports by buyers, Bloomberg reported on Monday, quoting a tender document it had seen. The Kuwaiti tender is a sign that the Gulf producers are hopeful that the Strait of Hormuz reopening would allow them to boost production and crude…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: EnCore OK’d to build South Dakota’s first ISR uranium mine
top_signals.summary: Dewey Burdock is expected to process 1 million lb. of uranium per year, recovering more than 14 million lb. over its 28-year life.
